name: Vadouvan-Spiced Roast Leg of Lamb
description: A slow-roasted semi-boneless leg of lamb coated in a vadouvan butter paste with orange zest, rosemary, and pink peppercorn. Halal French fusion, 4 hours total.
keywords: halal french cooking, vadouvan lamb, roast leg of lamb, halal roast
recipeIngredient: 225 g (2 sticks) unsalted halal butter, softened
1 semi-boneless leg of lamb, shank bone in, approximately 3.8–4.1 kg (8½–9 lb)

recipeInstructions.text: Remove the lamb from the refrigerator 1 hour before roasting.
Mix butter, vadouvan, orange zest, rosemary, salt, pink peppercorns, and black pepper into a smooth paste.
Preheat the oven to 160 °C (325 °F), conventional heat.
Rub half the paste under any loose flaps of fat; rub the remainder over the entire exterior of the lamb.
Place the lamb on a rack in a roasting tin and pour 240 ml water into the base of the tin.
Roast at 160 °C (325 °F) for 3 hours without opening the door.
Check internal temperature; it should read 60–63 °C (140–145 °F) at the thickest point.
Raise oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and roast for a further 20–25 minutes until the crust is deeply bronzed.
Rest the lamb tented in foil for at least 20 minutes before carving.
Deglaze the roasting tin over medium heat with a splash of stock or water; strain the pan juices and serve alongside.
